title: Boston runner to take on 2026 Marathon for Corey C. Griffin Foundation
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Annie Yardley, a Boston resident, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
Yardley, previously not athletic, reflects on personal growth through marathon training, challenging self-imposed labels.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation supports children's education and healthcare, inspiring Yardley's marathon participation.

title: Cape Cod man charged with sister’s murder
author: John Waller
bullets: Kenneth Beltrame, 67, was charged with the murder of his sister, Jennifer Beltrame, in Sandwich, Massachusetts.
Police found Jennifer Beltrame, 61, dead with signs of violent trauma after responding to a well-being check.
An investigation by Sandwich and State Police is ongoing, with Kenneth Beltrame expected to be arraigned in Barnstable District Court.

title: No one can use a gas-powered leaf blower in these 2 Mass. towns, thanks to new laws
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Arlington and Lexington ban gas-powered leaf blowers from March 15, allowing only electric models for leaf clearing.
Exceptions allow wheeled leaf blowers on large properties and gas-powered equipment during emergencies under new laws.
Violations in Arlington result in fines starting at $100, while Lexington fines start at $100 and increase with repeated offenses.

title: Fenway Park offers 4 new food concessions, including lobster poutine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Fenway Park introduces four new food concessions, including Lobstah Poutine, before Red Sox Opening Day.
Lobstah Poutine features Cavendish Farms fries, Luke's Lobster meat, bacon, and clam chowder.
New menu includes Surf & Turf Dog, mini empanadas, and spicy grilled cheese sandwiches.

title: 3 arrested in ‘highly coordinated’ break-in scheme that targeted Indian, East Asian residents
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three men arrested for a targeted break-in scheme against Indian and East Asian residents in Massachusetts.
David Rondon-Castro, Yeison Ramirez-Garcia, and Itan Dami Beltran used surveillance and electronic devices for the thefts.
The suspects allegedly stole cash, jewelry, and heirlooms valued at hundreds of thousands of dollars, authorities say.

title: Here’s where experts rank the Red Sox entering the 2026 season
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Red Sox ranked No. 7 by both Bleacher Report and MLB.com for the 2026 MLB season.
ESPN ranked Boston No. 9, highlighting Roman Anthony's performance and Garrett Crochet's Cy Young candidacy.
Boston Globe predictions favor Red Sox reaching postseason via A.L. wild card, but not winning the World Series.

title: Report: Celtics signing Charles Bassey to another 10-day contract
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics sign Charles Bassey to a second 10-day contract to provide front-court depth.
Bassey played two games with Celtics, previously played with Grizzlies and 76ers this season.
Nikola Vucevic is out with injury, Neemias Queta and Luka Garza fill starting and reserve roles.

title: Speakers, performers announced for ‘No Kings’ Boston rally Saturday
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey, Rep. Ayanna Pressley, and Dropkick Murphys set to appear at No Kings Boston rally Saturday.
The rally, scheduled from 2 to 4 p.m. on March 28 at Boston Common, may draw over 100,000 attendees.
Speakers will address issues like LGBTQIA+ rights and US aggression; a 24-foot 'END ICE' ice sculpture to be featured.

title: Report: Celtics executive Brad Stevens removes his name from UNC coaching search
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics executive Brad Stevens has withdrawn from consideration for the UNC head coaching position.
UNC is searching for a new head coach after firing Hubert Davis following an NCAA Tournament loss.
Stevens, a successful NBA executive, has been linked to high-profile college basketball coaching rumors.

title: Free Fenway Franks? Diner rolls out Opening Day deal for Red Sox fans.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: South Street Diner offers free all-you-can-eat Fenway Franks with a drink purchase on April 3 from 11 a.m. to 9 p.m.
Promotion coincides with Red Sox home opener against San Diego Padres at 2:10 p.m.; diner will broadcast the game.
Uber Eats orders over $20 from the diner between 2 p.m. and 6 p.m. on April 3 include a free Fenway Frank.
